title: euonymus occidentalis subsp. parishii
intro: Euonymus occidentalis subsp. parishii, also known as the California or Western Strawberry Tree, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, derived from its bark, leaves, and roots. The health benefits of Euonymus occidentalis subsp. parishii are said to include the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to act as a natural antiseptic. In herbal preparations, the plant's bark and roots are often made into teas, tinctures, and infusions to harness its medicinal properties.
